test(reasoning): TU arXiv:2603.01896 semi-formal reasoning — 30 new tests

Chapter 3 in test_suite.py, 3 test classes, 30 tests (56+30=86 total):

TestSemiFormalL1Prompt (4 tests):
  - Prompt structure: PREMISES/TRACE/VERDICT mandatory
  - arXiv:2603.01896 citation present
  - JSON schema includes premises[] and trace[] fields
  - Backward compat: score/issues/verdict retained

TestSemiFormalUnverifiedDetection (7 tests):
  - UNVERIFIED trace items surfaced as issues (no write tools)
  - Suppressed when agent used code_write
  - Suppressed when agent used code_edit
  - Capped at 3 items (noise control)
  - Empty/absent premises/trace: no crash, no spurious issues
  - Fully verified trace: zero UNVERIFIED issues

TestSemiFormalProtocols (12 tests):
  - QA protocol: premises/trace/conclusion present
  - QA protocol: arXiv ref, [APPROVE]/[VETO] verdicts
  - Review protocol: premises/trace/verdict present
  - Review protocol: arXiv ref, [APPROVE]/[REQUEST_CHANGES]
  - Semi-formal step precedes verdict in both protocols

TestSemiFormalRLM (7 tests):
  - _build_iteration_prompt: premises in final JSON schema
  - arXiv:2603.01896 citation in prompt
  - answer field alongside premises in final schema
  - search(): answer returned correctly with premises
  - search(): graceful without premises (backward compat)
  - search(): premises logged at DEBUG for audit
  - context_block variable (no backslash-in-fstring, py3.10 compat)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): fix asset upload failure on immutable release by bundling assets at creation (#1671)

- [x] Add `skip-github-release: true` to the `release-please` step
- [x] Add `body` output to the `release-please` job
- [x] Change `release-assets` job to upload compiled binaries as GitHub
Actions artifacts (using `actions/upload-artifact@v4`)
- [x] Reduce `release-assets` permissions to `contents: read` (no longer
needs write access)
- [x] Add new `publish-release` job that downloads all artifacts and
runs `gh release create` to create the release and upload assets
simultaneously
- [x] Add `docker` to `publish-release` job dependencies to prevent
creating a GitHub Release before the Docker image push completes
- [x] Pin `actions/upload-artifact` and `actions/download-artifact` to
full commit SHAs for supply-chain security consistency
- [x] Add `--verify-tag` to `gh release create` to prevent auto-creating
a release from the wrong commit if the tag doesn't exist upstream

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>CI/CD: Fix asset upload failure on immutable release by
bundling assets at creation</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> During the v3.0.0 release process, the GitHub Actions workflow failed
to upload binary assets with the error `HTTP 422: Cannot upload assets
to an immutable release`.
> This occurs because our repository has GitHub's "Immutable Releases"
security feature enabled. The `release-please` action immediately
publishes the release, making it immutable and preventing the subsequent
job from attaching the compiled assets.
> 
> ### Proposal
> We must embed the assets at the exact moment the release is created.
To achieve this, we will configure `release-please` to skip the GitHub
Release creation (while still generating the tag and changelog). Then,
after all assets are built across the matrix, a final job will use the
GitHub CLI (`gh release create`) to create the release and upload the
assets simultaneously.
> 
> ### Tasks
> - In the `release-please` step, add `skip-github-release: true`.
> - In the asset build job, upload the compiled binaries using
`actions/upload-artifact`.
> - Add a new final job (`publish-release`) that downloads all artifacts
and runs `gh release create` passing the asset files, the tag, and the
changelog body.
> 
> ### Example Workflow Update
> ```yaml
> jobs:
>   release-please:
>     runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
>     outputs:
>       release_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs.release_created }}
>       tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs.tag_name }}
>       body: ${{ steps.release.outputs.body }}
>     steps:
>       - id: generate_token
>         # ...
>       - id: release
>         uses: googleapis/release-please-action@v4
>         with:
>           token: ${{ steps.generate_token.outputs.token }}
>           skip-github-release: true # ★ 自動リリース作成をスキップ
> 
>   build-assets:
>     needs: release-please
>     if: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.release_created == 'true' }}
>     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
>     # ... (matrix setup and go build) ...
>     steps:
>       # ...
>       - name: Upload artifact
>         uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
>         with:
>           name: assets-${{ matrix.os_label }}-${{ matrix.arch_label }}
>           path: manael_*.tar.gz
> 
>   publish-release:
>     needs: [release-please, build-assets]
>     if: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.release_created == 'true' }}
>     runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
>     steps:
>       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
>       - name: Download all assets
>         uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
>         with:
>           path: dist
>           pattern: assets-*
>           merge-multiple: true
>       - name: Create Immutable Release
>         env:
>           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
>           RELEASE_NOTES: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.body }}
>         run: |
> gh release create "${{ needs.release-please.outputs.tag_name }}" \
>             dist/* \
> --title "Release ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.tag_name }}" \
>             --notes "$RELEASE_NOTES"
> ```</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

- Fixes manaelproxy/manael#1670

-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
* Release bodies are now captured and propagated so published releases
include full release notes.
* CI uploads build artifacts which are later downloaded and attached to
releases.
* Release creation consolidated into a dedicated publish step that
assembles artifacts and publishes the release.
* Release notes passed into the publish step to populate release
content.
* Asset handling and permissions tightened; an option added to skip
automated release publishing.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
feat(reasoning): semi-formal reasoning arXiv:2603.01896

Premises → Trace → Verdict in L1 adversarial reviewer, QA/review protocols,
and RLM final answer. Certificate-style reasoning prevents skipping cases and
surfacing unsupported claims.

- adversarial.py L1: prompt requires premises[]+trace[] before score/verdict;
  UNVERIFIED trace items surfaced as hallucination signal (write-tool aware)
- engine.py QA protocol: step 5 = Premises/Trace/Conclusion before APPROVE/VETO
- engine.py review protocol: semi-formal before APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES
- rlm.py: final answer action requires premises[] field (every claim cites
  a specific finding); log at DEBUG for traceability; fix nested f-string \n
- CLAUDE.md: AGENTIC CODE REASONING section with design choices

Backward-compatible: premises/trace fields optional, older LLMs still work.
REF: arXiv:2603.01896 — patch equivalence 78→93%, fault loc +5pp, QA 87%.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: escape Liquid tags in navigation.md code examples

Jekyll processes Liquid template syntax before rendering markdown,
so the code examples in navigation.md were being executed:

  {% assign ordered = nav_pages | ... | sort: "order" %}
  {% assign unordered = nav_pages | ... | sort: "title" %}

Since nav_pages is undefined in this page context, the sort filter
received nil and raised: Cannot sort a null object. (ArgumentError)

Wrap the liquid code block with {% raw %}...{% endraw %} so Jekyll
passes it through unprocessed to the markdown renderer.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: anchor release detection to GitHub API, ban shallow-clone git tag (#17)

* fix: anchor release detection to GitHub API, ban shallow-clone git tag

The create-release agent was calling `git tag` in a shallow clone
(--depth=1 --no-tags), receiving empty results, and concluding there
were no existing releases. It then ignored its own `gh release list`
output and created v0.1.0 instead of the correct next version.

Changes:
- Step 1: Replace vague instruction with explicit `gh release list`
  command and a hard ban on git-based release detection
- Step 3: Add guard requiring the new version to be strictly greater
  than the last release tag, with a concrete example

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(F-004): 명령군별 JSON 입력 마이그레이션

- F-004-C1: build/spec/db/test/runner/config 명령군이 invoke를 통한
  JSON payload 입력으로 동일 기능 수행 (JsonCommand.cs RouteRequest 경로)

- F-004-C2: 명령 실행 전 유효성 검사 추가
  * ValidateRequiredFields: 필수 필드 누락 사전 검출
  * ValidateOptionTypes: 타입 불일치 사전 검출

Improve web search tools: reliability, efficiency, and content extraction

WebSearchService:
- Add retry logic with 1.5s delay on failure
- Add fallback to lite.duckduckgo.com when primary endpoint fails
- Add CookieContainer + compression for DDG anti-bot handling
- Add alternate snippet regex for better snippet extraction
- Filter out DuckDuckGo's own links from results

WebFetchService:
- Restructure extraction pipeline: extract content FIRST, strip noise AFTER
- Prioritize <article> over <main> for cleaner content isolation
- Add ARIA role-based noise stripping (navigation, toolbar, menubar, etc.)
- Add class/id-based noise stripping (sidebar, toc, breadcrumb, share, etc.)
- Save full content to temp file, return 8K inline preview + file path
- Smart truncation: scan remaining text for headings and list cut sections
- Strip boilerplate text (Focus mode, Ask Learn, Share via, feedback, etc.)
- Convert headings to markdown, list items to bullets for structure
- Auto-retry on transient errors (408/429/5xx/timeout)
- Auto-cleanup temp files older than 1 hour

New lumi_research tool:
- Combined search + auto-fetch in a single tool call (replaces 3-6 calls)
- Searches then fetches top 3 results in parallel
- Skips failed fetches (403, timeout) and tries next results automatically
- Registered in tools, system prompt, agent tools, and display helpers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: override sidebar-nav.html to use single-line nav_pages assignment

The remote theme's sidebar-nav.html used a multi-line Liquid assign
for nav_pages which caused Liquid 4.0.4 to assign nil, making
subsequent where_exp | sort chains fail with:
  'Cannot sort a null object. (ArgumentError)'

Override with a single-line assignment that is safe in all Liquid versions.

commit.message:
feat(tools): /workspace path alias → project workspace

Agents can now use /workspace/src/main.py instead of the internal
/app/data/workspaces/<project-id>/src/main.py path.

- Extract _resolve_file_path(path, project_path) as pure function
- /workspace  and /workspace/  →  project_path
- /workspace/<rest>  →  project_path/<rest>
- Other absolute paths (other projects, /tmp, /etc…) pass through unchanged
  (security enforcement stays in code_tools._is_path_allowed)

  path traversal attempt, edge cases (partial prefix, no project_path)

feat(skills): add description + triggers to all 120 skills (batch)

- 103/121 skills missing 'description' field
- 56/121 skills missing 'triggers' field

Batch fixes:
- description: derived from tagline+role, or first sentence of persona.description,
  or motivation. Max 120 chars. Role-specific wording preserved.
- triggers: 3 natural-language 'when X' entries per skill, matched to role family
  (dev/arch/security/data/product/qa/platform/rse/finops/incident/...).
  56 skills received triggers. Skills with existing triggers (65) untouched.

All 120 YAMLs parse cleanly. 0 errors.

WHY: description drives agent selection UI + stocktake verdicts.
     triggers enable auto-routing without manual agent assignment.

commit.message:
Rewrite as composite action using bash and gh CLI

Replace the Docker-based Go implementation with a lightweight composite
action that uses a bash script and the gh CLI. This eliminates the
Docker cold start, removes ~180 lines of Go code, and simplifies
maintenance.

Key changes:
- Replace Docker + Go with a composite action running close.sh
- Convert AC_LABEL/AC_KEEP env vars to proper action inputs (label/keep)
- Use gh CLI for issue listing and closing (handles auth and pagination)
- Update README with new usage instructions and input documentation

Callers need to update from env-based config to inputs:
  with: { label: '...', keep: N }
  env: { GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} }

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: auto-closer
repository.description: A GitHub Action that closes all issues with a specific label while keeping the latest one(s) open.

commit.message:
fix: parseable ERR emission, named arg round-trip, \U escape support (#548)

- CalorEmitter: emit ERR "TODO: ..." instead of ERR{"TODO:..."} to prevent
  cascading Calor0100 parse errors (FallbackExpressionNode)
- CalorEmitter: emit named argument labels A[name] in Visit(CallStatementNode)
  and Visit(CallExpressionNode) when ArgumentNames are present
- Parser: add ParseArgument(out argumentName) overload that handles A[name]
  syntax for named arguments; collect names in both statement and expression
  call parsing
- Lexer: add \UXXXXXXXX (8-digit) Unicode escape support via
  AppendLongUnicodeEscape(), applied in both triple-quoted and regular strings
- Tests: 22 new tests in ConverterQualityTests.cs covering ERR parsing,
  Unicode escapes, named arg round-trip, and real-world patterns
- Update existing assertion in CSharpToCalorConversionTests for new ERR format

Addresses cascading parse failures reported in Newtonsoft.Json (269 failures)
and Humanizer (72 failures) conversion reports.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: calor
repository.description: Calor - Coding Agent Language for Optimized Reasoning

commit.message:
fix: use force reset for submodule updates to avoid merge conflicts

These submodules are read-only upstream sources. Using --merge can cause
conflicts when upstream rewrites history or has divergent changes. Reset
each submodule to its remote HEAD before updating to avoid conflicts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use global git config so submodule merges have identity

git submodule update --remote --merge performs merges inside submodule
directories, which don't inherit the repo-local git config. Using
--global ensures the committer identity is available everywhere.

alone means the Lint and Test stages cannot find those tools and fail.

commit.message:
fix: keep action bar always mounted to prevent message width shrink on hover

The action toolbar child was being dynamically mounted/unmounted based on
hover state via UpdateActionChromeMount(). Since the toolbar sits in an
Auto-sized Grid column, removing its child caused the column to collapse
to 0 width, making the star-sized bubble column expand. On hover, the
child was re-added, restoring the Auto column width and shrinking the
bubble — causing a visible layout jump.

Fix: always keep the action bar child mounted. The XAML styles already
control visibility through Opacity and IsHitTestVisible on :pointerover,
:host-scrolling, and :editing pseudo-classes, so the mount/unmount
optimization is unnecessary and actively harmful to layout stability.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(llm): return synthetic neutral response on Azure HTTP 400 content policy

When Azure OpenAI blocks a prompt with content management policy (HTTP
400), return a neutral '[CONTENT_POLICY_BLOCKED]' completion instead of
raising an exception that triggers circuit breaker + infinite retry loop.

Affects all three call paths: chat completions, Responses API, streaming.
Particularly relevant for the ac-adversarial agent whose adversarial
prompts sometimes trigger Azure content filters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: video-2022
repository.description: 自研视频点播平台

commit.message:
fix: remove cache-first service worker causing stale pages

Root cause: sw.js used cache-first strategy — old HTML pages cached by SW
were served forever, ignoring new deployments. Users clearing browser cache
doesn't affect SW cache, so old landing page persisted across all deploys.

Fix:
- Replace sw.js with self-destructing version (clears all caches + unregisters)
- ServiceWorkerRegister now unregisters SW and clears caches on all visits
- Existing users' old SW will fetch new sw.js (skipWaiting) → auto-cleanup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
